summarization.a: The heatmap shows how the mapped value varies with TMA flow rate and HF flow rate. A pronounced high-value region appears at low HF flow rates and low TMA flow rates. Outside this region, values remain low across most of the parameter space, indicating a localized dependence on both parameters.

a.question: How does the mapped value vary across HF and TMA flow rates in this heatmap?
a.answer: The mapped value is highest at low HF and low TMA flow rates, forming a localized high-value region. As either flow rate increases, the value drops sharply and remains low across most of the remaining parameter space.

a.question: Which two process parameters are shown on the axes of the heatmap?
a.answer: TMA flow rate and HF flow rate.

a.question: Is the high-value region spread broadly across the heatmap?
At which parameter combination are the highest values observed?
a.answer: Low HF flow rate, Low TMA flow rate
